subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — EOG Practice Test 5
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer. For gridded questions, enter just the number. For fill-in-the-blank equations, enter the number that makes the equation true.

questions.questionText: What multiplication fact does this array of dots show?
questions.explanation: There are 4 rows of 7 dots: 4 × 7 = 28.

questions.questionText: Which number is shown by these base-ten blocks?
questions.explanation: 4 hundreds, 2 tens, and 0 ones make 420.

questions.questionText: What is 67 rounded to the nearest ten?
questions.explanation: 67 is closer to 70 than to 60, so it rounds to 70.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: 3 of the 4 equal parts are shaded, so 3/4.

questions.questionText: What time does the clock show?
questions.explanation: The hour hand is halfway between 2 and 3 and the minute hand points to 6, which is 30 minutes, so 2:30.

questions.questionText: What is 348 + 215? Enter just the number.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
What is 54 ÷ 9?
Which comparison is true?
questions.explanation: 2/3 has more shaded parts than 1/3, so 2/3 > 1/3.

questions.questionText: How many sides does this shape have?
questions.explanation: A pentagon has 5 sides.

questions.questionText: How many books did Mia read?
questions.explanation: Mia's bar reaches 8 on the scale.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point on the number line show?
questions.explanation: The number line is split into 4 equal parts and the point is at the third mark, which is 3/4.

questions.questionText: What fraction of this bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: 5 of the 6 equal parts are shaded, so 5/6.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
A teacher shares 72 stickers equally among 8 students. How many stickers does each student get? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: 72 ÷ 8 = 9 stickers each.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equivalent to 1/3?
questions.explanation: 2/6 covers the same amount as 1/3, so they are equivalent.

questions.questionText: Music class starts at the time shown and lasts 25 minutes. What time does it end?
questions.explanation: 10:15 plus 25 minutes is 10:40.

questions.questionText: A rug is 8 feet long and 5 feet wide. What is its area, in square feet? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: Area = 8 × 5 = 40 square feet.

questions.questionText: Each apple stands for 5 pieces of fruit picked. How many pieces did Class B pick?
questions.explanation: 7 apples × 5 = 35 pieces of fruit.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Select all the shapes that are quadrilaterals (shapes with exactly 4 sides).
questions.explanation: A square and a rhombus each have exactly 4 sides. A pentagon has 5 sides and a triangle has 3.

questions.questionText: Which comparison is true?
questions.explanation: With the same denominator, more shaded parts means more: 4/6 > 3/6.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point on the number line show?
questions.explanation: The number line is split into 4 equal parts and the point is at the second mark, which is 2/4.

questions.questionText: There are 8 fish in a tank. 3 of them are orange. What fraction of the fish are orange?
questions.explanation: 3 out of 8 fish are orange, so 3/8.

questions.questionText: What is the value of the digit 7 in the number 736? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: The 7 is in the hundreds place, so its value is 700.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Which expression has the same value as 7 × 6?
questions.explanation: Multiplication can be done in any order, so 6 × 7 = 7 × 6 = 42.

questions.questionText: In the counting pattern 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, ..., select all the numbers that belong in the pattern.
questions.explanation: The pattern is multiples of 4. 24 and 32 are multiples of 4; 30 and 26 are not.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 56 ÷ 8 = 7, so the blank is 8.

questions.questionText: A gardener planted 4 rows of tulips with 9 tulips in each row. Then 12 tulips were picked. How many tulips are left?
questions.explanation: 4 × 9 = 36 tulips; 36 − 12 = 24 tulips left.

questions.questionText: Each ticket costs $7. Dana buys 5 tickets and pays with a $50 bill. How much change does she get back?
A rectangle has an area of 56 square centimeters. If one side is 8 centimeters long, how long is the other side, in centimeters? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: Area = length × width, so 56 ÷ 8 = 7 cm.

questions.questionText: What is the perimeter, in units, of this rectangle?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 6 + 4 + 6 + 4 = 20 units. (Area would be 24, a common trap.)

questions.questionText: Recess started at 11:50 and ended at 12:20. How long did recess last?
questions.explanation: From 11:50 to 12:00 is 10 minutes, and 12:00 to 12:20 is 20 minutes: 10 + 20 = 30 minutes.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is the greatest?
questions.explanation: When the top number is 1, the smaller the bottom number, the larger the fraction. 1/2 is the greatest because 2 is the smallest denominator.

questions.questionText: Select all the expressions that equal 24.
Select all the fractions that are equal to 1/2.
questions.explanation: 3/6, 2/4, and 4/8 each equal 1/2. 2/5 is less than 1/2.

questions.questionText: Select all the numbers that round to 600 when rounded to the nearest hundred.
questions.explanation: 552, 649, and 647 each round to 600. 548 rounds to 500.

questions.questionText: A baker made 7 trays of muffins with 6 muffins on each tray. She sold 23 muffins. How many muffins are left? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: 7 × 6 = 42 muffins made; 42 − 23 = 19 left.
